subtopics: Need for measurement, Units of measurement, Systems of units
key_concepts: SI units (fundamental and derived), Significant figures, Uncertainty in result, Dimensions of physical quantities, Dimensional analysis and applications

subtopics: Frame of reference, Motion description using differentiation and integration
key_concepts: Uniform and non-uniform motion, Average speed/velocity vs Instantaneous velocity, Uniformly accelerated motion (graphical & calculus), Velocity-time graphs, Position-time graphs

subtopics: Vector quantities and operations, Motion types in plane
key_concepts: Position/displacement vectors, Vector addition/subtraction/multiplication by scalar, Unit vector, Resolution of vectors (rectangular components), Scalar and Vector products, Projectile motion, Uniform circular motion

subtopics: Newton's Laws, Momentum and Impulse, Friction
key_concepts: Force, Inertia (1st Law), Momentum & 2nd Law, Impulse, Conservation of linear momentum, Equilibrium of concurrent forces, Static/Kinetic/Rolling friction, Lubrication, Dynamics of uniform circular motion (Centripetal force)

subtopics: Work done by forces, Energy forms, Collisions
key_concepts: Work (constant/variable force), Kinetic energy, Work-energy theorem, Power, Potential energy (spring), Conservative vs Non-conservative forces, Motion in vertical circle, Elastic and Inelastic collisions (1D & 2D)

units.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
explicit_chapters: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

chapters.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
chapters.source_chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
chapters.source_heading: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
subtopics: Center of Mass, Torque & Angular Momentum, Rigid Body Rotation
key_concepts: CM of 2-particle system and rigid bodies (uniform rod), Conservation of momentum/CM motion, Moment of force/Torque/Angular momentum, Equilibrium of rigid bodies, Rotational equations of motion vs Linear motions comparison, Moment of inertia & Radius of gyration

subtopics: Planetary motion, Universal gravitation, Gravity variations
key_concepts: Kepler's laws, Universal law of gravitation, Acceleration due to gravity (variation with altitude/depth), Gravitational potential energy & potential, Escape speed, Orbital velocity and energy of satellites
Stress-strain relationship, Hooke's law, Young’s modulus, Bulk modulus, Shear modulus (qualitative), Poisson's ratio, Elastic energy, Applications of elastic behavior (qualitative)

subtopics: Fluid pressure and flow, Surface tension
key_concepts: Pressure due to fluid column (Pascal's law), Viscosity & Stokes' law, Terminal velocity, Streamline/Turbulent flow, Bernoulli's theorem (Torricelli's law/Dynamic lift), Surface energy/tension, Angle of contact, Excess pressure across curved surface

subtopics: Heat, Temperature and Expansion, Heat Transfer
key_concepts: Thermal expansion (solids/liquids/gases), Anomalous expansion of water, Specific heat capacity (Cp/Cv), Calorimetry, Change of state/Latent heat, Conduction/Convection/Radiation, Thermal conductivity, Blackbody radiation concepts
Thermal equilibrium/Zeroth law, Heat/Work/Internal energy, First Law, Second Law (state variables/equation of state), Isothermal/Adiabatic/Reversible/Irreversible/Cyclic processes

units.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

key_concepts: Equation of state, Work done in compression, Kinetic assumptions/Pressure concept, Temperature interpretation/rms speed, Degrees of freedom/Law of equipartition (statement), Specific heat capacities, Mean free path, Avogadro's number
Time period, Frequency, Displacement function/periodic functions, Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M), Uniform circular motion relation to S.H.M

subtopics: Wave characteristics and phenomena (implied by unit title)
key_concepts: Basic wave properties, Wave motion types
Coulomb's law-force between two-point charges
Forces between multiple charges; superposition principle and continuous charge distribution
Electric field, electric field due to a point charge, electric field lines
Equipotential surfaces
Electrical potential energy of a system of two-point charges and of electric dipole in an electrostatic field
Conductors and insulators, free charges and bound charges inside a conductor
Electric current, flow of electric charges in a metallic conductor, drift velocity, mobility and their relation with electric current

units.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

key_concepts: Concept of magnetic field, Oersted's experiment
Bar magnet, bar magnet as an equivalent solenoid (qualitative treatment only)
